notes: Rux is the source of truth for agent token discipline.
Agents should summarize oversized command output, keep full logs outside model context, and create handoffs before long sessions become the operating default.
Use expensive models, high effort, and subagents only when the task class and expected artifact justify the extra context.
Provider CLIs use the user's installed auth and config.
Do not fan out parallel runs over one subscription-backed CLI account by default.
Export summaries omit transcript text unless explicitly requested.
Improvement loops may propose changes, but must not apply them silently.
Token-governor policy is advisory. Rux-wrapped provider runs cap visible provider output; broader shell-output brokering and session lifecycle enforcement are future work.
